content.heading: Session 4 Capstone — build the display logic

blocks.text: You now have all the tools to build the core of the Simon Says game. This capstone brings together arrays, loops, random picks, and callbacks into two functions that actually run the display phase.

blocks.label: What you are building
blocks.text: pickCells(n) picks n unique random cells from the grid. startRound() calls pickCells, logs the sequence, then after 1500ms logs that the lights are off and it is the player's turn. This is the exact logic the real game uses.

exercises.title: Build pickCells and startRound
tasks: Write pickCells(n) — returns n unique random numbers from 0 to 8. No duplicates.
Write startRound() — calls pickCells(level), stores the result in litCells, logs 'Lighting up: ' + litCells, then after 1500ms logs 'Lights off. Your turn.'
Declare state variables at the top: litCells = [], level = 2.
Call it three times and confirm a different sequence appears each time.
exercises.hint: pickCells: start with all 9 indexes, pick one randomly, splice it out, repeat. startRound: use setTimeout to delay the 'lights off' message.

checks.label: pickCells(3) returns an array of length 3
